commands.repair_reason: - [P2] The blocker is an API trust-model decision: maintainers must choose plugin-bound identity versus explicit operator-scoped trust before automation can safely repair or merge the public SDK/protocol surface.; Needs attention: Verified allow resolution is presented as plugin-owned but is authorized by caller-supplied pluginId over a generic operator approvals client.; Review finding: [P1] Bind verified resolutio... (sha=f21398cdb856c0129ea8757e8b8df8a6ca0ec7a6)
commands.review_summary:
The branch adds `externalResolution` metadata for plugin approvals, a new `plugin.approval.resolveVerified` Gateway/protocol/SDK path, and renderer/docs/tests for external verification commands.

PR surface: Source +801, Tests +961, Docs +51, Generated 0, Other +26. Total +1839 across 49 files.

Reproducibility: yes. Source inspection gives a high-confidence path: create an external-resolution approval for plugin A, connect a generic trusted approval-runtime client, and call `plugin.approval.resolveVerified` with plugin A's id because the handler trusts request params.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] The blocker is an API trust-model decision: maintainers must choose plugin-bound identity versus explicit operator-scoped trust before automation can safely repair or merge the public SDK/protocol surface.

target.title: feat: add external plugin approval verification
target.body:
## Summary

This is the narrowed AgentKit host API slice after @kevinslin's feedback about avoiding a broad custom-action surface.

- Adds `externalResolution` for plugin approvals as a single command-template field, not arbitrary custom action arrays.
- Keeps core as the normal approval owner: OpenClaw still renders core decisions from `allowedDecisions`, and AgentKit uses only core `deny` there.
- Adds `plugin.approval.resolveVerified` for proof-backed allow resolution, scoped to the originating `pluginId` and registered behind `operator.approvals` (`operator.admin` still satisfies it as the normal operator-scope superset).
- Teaches approval view models, text/channel renderers, Control UI parsing, protocol schema, docs, and SDK types to carry/render the narrow external verification command.
- Rejects mixed normal allow decisions with external verification, so allow-once/allow-always must go through the verified plugin-owned resolver.

This replaces the earlier broader approach from #82431. The chat/status-card follow-up from #82471 is not required for the current AgentKit end-to-end path.

## Review context

This PR is the narrower replacement for the earlier custom-action approach. It does **not** add arbitrary plugin-defined approval actions.

The concrete use case is the external AgentKit plugin:

- https://github.com/Guardiola31337/openclaw-agentkit
- https://github.com/Guardiola31337/openclaw-agentkit/pull/2
- https://clawhub.ai/plugins/@guardiola31337/agentkit

AgentKit needs to let a user complete external World verification before a protected OpenClaw tool continues, while keeping OpenClaw core as the approval owner.

```mermaid
flowchart LR
  A[Agent calls protected tool] --> B[OpenClaw creates plugin approval]
  B --> C[OpenClaw renders normal approval prompt]
  C --> D[User chooses external verification command]
  D --> E[AgentKit starts World verification]
  E --> F[World proof succeeds]
  F --> G[AgentKit calls resolveVerified]
  G --> H[OpenClaw checks plugin ownership]
  H --> I[Tool call continues]
```

## Trust boundary

```mermaid
flowchart TB
  subgraph Core[OpenClaw core]
    A[Owns pending approval]
    B[Renders normal deny and timeout handling]
    C[Validates approval id]
    D[Checks originating plugin id]
    E[Resolves approval]
  end

  subgraph Plugin[AgentKit plugin]
    F[Owns World verification]
    G[Shows QR/link]
    H[Submits proof-backed allow]
  end

  A --> F
  H --> D
  D --> E
```

## Why this shape

The earlier approach exposed arbitrary `actions`, which made every approval-capable client understand plugin-defined buttons. That was too broad for this use case.

This PR keeps the core approval contract intact and adds only:

- `externalResolution`: a narrow command-template hint for external verification.
- `plugin.approval.resolveVerified`: an ownership-checked resolver so plugin X can resolve only approvals created by plugin X.

Core still owns pending approval state, timeout, deny handling, and normal approval rendering. AgentKit owns only the external World verification step and can only resolve approvals that originated from AgentKit.

## Verification

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/gateway/method-scopes.test.ts src/gateway/operator-approvals-client.test.ts src/gateway/server-methods/plugin-approval.test.ts` (9 files, 416 tests)
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/plugin-sdk/approval-renderers.test.ts` (1 file, 6 tests)
- `node scripts/docs-list.js`
- `pnpm build`
- `git diff --check`
- AgentKit companion plugin checkout linked to this branch:
  - `pnpm test:hitl`
  - `pnpm test:openclaw-hitl`
  - `pnpm test:local-full-e2e -- --openclaw ../openclaw --skip-host-build --skip-link`

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: external HITL plugins can ask OpenClaw to show a narrow verification command for allow decisions while core keeps denial and pending approval ownership.

Real environment tested: local OpenClaw checkout on Node 22 using focused gateway/plugin approval tests, SDK renderer tests, full build, docs listing, and a companion AgentKit plugin gateway e2e run against this host API.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: ran the commands listed in `Verification` after rebasing on latest `origin/main`, then re-ran the AgentKit wrapper against this linked OpenClaw checkout.

Evidence after fix: copied after-fix output from the AgentKit local OpenClaw gateway e2e run:

```json
{
  "ok": true,
  "denyApprovalCommands": [
    "/agentkit approve plugin:5f073ff9-7ca7-45ba-bf57-d920909973ea allow-once",
    "/agentkit approve plugin:5f073ff9-7ca7-45ba-bf57-d920909973ea allow-always",
    "/approve plugin:5f073ff9-7ca7-45ba-bf57-d920909973ea deny"
  ],
  "deniedHookResult": {
    "blocked": true,
    "kind": "failure",
    "deniedReason": "plugin-approval",
    "reason": "Denied by user",
    "params": {
      "cmd": "echo agentkit"
    }
  },
  "allowApprovalCommands": [
    "/agentkit approve plugin:111f0504-d4d8-4ee5-bd80-57bce3e8525a allow-once",
    "/agentkit approve plugin:111f0504-d4d8-4ee5-bd80-57bce3e8525a allow-always",
    "/approve plugin:111f0504-d4d8-4ee5-bd80-57bce3e8525a deny"
  ],
  "allowedHookResult": {
    "blocked": false,
    "params": {
      "cmd": "echo agentkit-allow"
    },
    "approvalResolution": "allow-once"
  }
}
```

Observed result after fix: plugin approval prompts render external verification as command text, core deny remains available, verified allow resolution is approval-scoped and plugin-owned, and the companion AgentKit plugin can continue the protected call after proof-backed `allow-once`.

What was not tested: a live World App QR scan from the TUI in this OpenClaw PR branch. The companion AgentKit plugin branch covers the plugin-level HITL and local OpenClaw gateway e2e path.
target.author: guardiola31337

commands.author: Guardiola31337
commands.review_summary:
The PR changes `scripts/ios-team-id.sh` to parse Xcode team data from `defaults export com.apple.dt.Xcode -` and updates the iOS team-id tests.

PR surface: Tests +57, Other +35. Total +92 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. from source inspection and PR proof. Current main lacks the identifier-key reader, and the PR body includes macOS/Xcode before-and-after terminal output for the helper and signing caller; I did not run a live macOS repro in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] A repair worker can make the existing PR mergeable by adding real-parser regression coverage and refreshing the branch; maintainer judgment is only needed if the source-order compatibility change should be rejected.

target.title: fix(ios): read IDEProvisioningTeamByIdentifier
target.body:
> UPDATE: created this PR while trying out the openclaw iOS app. Everything worked out of the box except of `scripts/ios-team-id.sh`. So I thought this tiny fix might be of use. Reg. agents feedback about tests: Didn't wrote tests that execute python code since there were no existing ones that do that.

## Summary

- Problem: `scripts/ios-team-id.sh` only read `IDEProvisioningTeams` and legacy fallbacks. On my local Xcode prefs, the team list is stored under `IDEProvisioningTeamByIdentifier`, so team lookup failed.
- Why it matters: `scripts/ios-configure-signing.sh` then exits with `WARN: Unable to detect an Apple Team ID; keeping existing iOS signing override (if any).`
- What changed: parse exported Xcode prefs for both `IDEProvisioningTeamByIdentifier` and `IDEProvisioningTeams`, and add test coverage for the identifier-key layout.
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): team selection preference order and the existing provisioning-profile / keychain fallbacks.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Refactor
- [ ] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [ ] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [ ] Integrations
- [ ] API / contracts
- [x] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #
- Related #22773

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

- `scripts/ios-team-id.sh` now resolves team IDs from Xcode prefs that store teams under `IDEProvisioningTeamByIdentifier`.
- `scripts/ios-configure-signing.sh` now succeeds on that prefs layout instead of warning and leaving signing unchanged.

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? (`Yes/No`) No
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- New/changed network calls? (`Yes/No`) No
- Command/tool execution surface changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- Data access scope changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation:

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: macOS
- Runtime/container: local shell
- Model/provider: N/A
- Integration/channel (if any): N/A
- Relevant config (redacted): `IOS_PYTHON_BIN=/usr/bin/python3` for deterministic local repro

### Steps

1. Save the pre-change `scripts/ios-team-id.sh` and `scripts/ios-configure-signing.sh` from `HEAD` to a temp directory and run `IOS_PYTHON_BIN=/usr/bin/python3 .../ios-configure-signing.sh`.
2. Run `IOS_PYTHON_BIN=/usr/bin/python3 scripts/ios-team-id.sh` from this branch.
3. Run `IOS_PYTHON_BIN=/usr/bin/python3 scripts/ios-configure-signing.sh` from this branch.
4. Run `pnpm exec vitest test/scripts/ios-team-id.test.ts`.

### Expected

- Before the fix, the old helper should fail to resolve a team ID on this Xcode prefs layout.
- After the fix, the helper should print the selected Team ID and signing configuration should succeed.
- The regression test should pass.

### Actual

- Before: `WARN: Unable to detect an Apple Team ID; keeping existing iOS signing override (if any).`
- After `scripts/ios-team-id.sh`: `2FD7P95TP8`
- After `scripts/ios-configure-signing.sh`: `iOS signing config already up to date: team=2FD7P95TP8 app=ai.openclaw.ios.test.klem-2fd7p95tp8`
- Test: `pnpm exec vitest test/scripts/ios-team-id.test.ts`

## Evidence

Attach at least one:

- [x] Failing test/log before + passing after
- [x] Trace/log snippets
- [ ] Screenshot/recording
- [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant)

## Human Verification (required)

What you personally verified (not just CI), and how:

- Verified scenarios:
  - Local Xcode prefs contain `IDEProvisioningTeamByIdentifier` with valid teams.
  - The pre-change helper fails on that prefs layout.
  - The patched helper returns `2FD7P95TP8` on the same machine.
  - `scripts/ios-configure-signing.sh` succeeds with the patched helper.
  - `pnpm exec vitest test/scripts/ios-team-id.test.ts` passes.
- Edge cases checked:
  - Existing team de-duplication stays in place via the unchanged `append_team` logic.
- What you did **not** verify:
  - Physical-device build/install after this script-only change.

## Review Conversations

- [x] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [x] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? (`Yes/No`) Yes
- Config/env changes? (`Yes/No`) No
- Migration needed? (`Yes/No`) No
- If yes, exact upgrade steps:

## Failure Recovery (if this breaks)

- How to disable/revert this change quickly: revert this PR or set `IOS_DEVELOPMENT_TEAM` explicitly.
- Files/config to restore: `scripts/ios-team-id.sh`
- Known bad symptoms reviewers should watch for: `scripts/ios-team-id.sh` prints no team ID on a machine where `IDEProvisioningTeamByIdentifier` is populated.

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: parsing both Xcode prefs keys could surface duplicate team entries.
  - Mitigation: `append_team` already de-duplicates by Team ID before selection.
target.author: fetzig

commands.reason: repair commands require a ClawSweeper PR or a PR opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
